hero.subtitle: Technical partner for FTTH projects in Germany. We coordinate field teams, document with rigor and deliver traceable work: from distribution network to final connection.
hero.title5: Field coordination, documentation and clean handover

values.title: Why Nexus
values.subtitle: We don't sell technology. We deliver verifiable work: completed connections, complete documentation and clean handover.
values.description: Each project has a known responsible party, visible status and documented closeout. This is what sets us apart.

why.planning: We plan with real data, not empty spreadsheets. Every segment, every connection, every team has a clear assignment.
why.field: Field work is our daily business. In-house or coordinated teams, familiar with German standards and defined processes.
why.documentation: Documentation is not an add-on. It's part of every closeout: photos, status, field notes and handover so there's no room for interpretation.
why.coordination: We coordinate teams, appointments and priorities. We don't expect the client to solve logistics problems.

ne3.title: Regional NE3 Distribution
ne3.description: Bringing fiber from the existing distribution network to each building's handover point. Work includes blowing, splicing at POP and intermediate points, measurements and documentation so the network is ready for NE4.

detail.intro: Problem: getting fiber to each building when the distribution network exists but doesn't reach the handover point. Work: blow fiber, splice at POP and intermediate points, measure per spec. Result: documented network ready to connect final customers.
detail.step1: Segment preparation: review plans, involved POP/DP, ducts, chambers, cabinets, permits/access and real site conditions before intervention.
detail.step2: Blowing/pulling: prepare ducts, lubrication/guide where applicable, blow fiber/cable and control the route according to design.
detail.step3: Network splicing and order: splices in POP, DP, closures or cabinets, fiber identification and physical organization for operations.
detail.step4: Measurement and closeout: tests where in scope, photos, incidents, as-built/redlines, open points and technical handover.
detail.output1: Segment record with location, status, photos and technical notes.
detail.output2: Open items separated by cause: access, material, damage, civil work, documentation or client coordination.
detail.output3: Closeout package for QA, internal certification, NE4 continuity or billing preparation.
detail.signal1: Every segment has a clear state: ready, partial, blocked or pending third parties.
detail.signal2: Splices, measurements and photos are linked to the correct physical point.
detail.signal3: The handover prevents the next crew from reconstructing the story in WhatsApp.

ne4.title: NE4 final FTTH connection
ne4.description: Connecting the final customer to the existing handover point. We check access, execute the indoor drop where applicable, mount the terminal equipment and document with photos and clear status for QA or second visit.

detail.intro: NE4 is the final connection inside the customer environment. What matters is validating access and route, working from DP/HÜP to GF-TA/ONT where applicable, terminating correctly and leaving clear evidence for QA, second visit or billing.
detail.step1: Validate appointment, address, access, building type, DP/HÜP and feasible indoor route.
detail.step2: Prepare the indoor drop or relevant segment: entry point, conduit/drilling where applicable and route protection.
detail.step3: Mount or prepare GF-TA/ONT with splice/connectorization and technical check before closeout.
detail.step4: Close with photos, blocker notes, visit evidence and final status for QA, billing or second visit.
detail.output1: Address-level report with DP/HÜP, internal route, GF-TA/ONT, before/after photos and visit result.
detail.output2: Open points classified by cause: no access, customer absent, blocked route, missing material, civil work, damage or activation out of scope.
detail.output3: Technical closeout ready for coordination: completed, partial, blocked or second visit required.
detail.signal1: The DP → HÜP → GF-TA → ONT chain is visible instead of buried in loose notes.
detail.signal2: QA photos connect each physical component to the right address and technician.
detail.signal3: Statuses allow second visits to be planned without WhatsApp archaeology.

workManager.title: Work Manager
workManager.description: Internal PWA for fiber operations: appointments, team PINs, offline queue, field reports, PDF/Excel export and project status flow.

detail.intro: Work Manager is the internal PWA for NE4 field teams. It centralizes appointments, technician PIN access, reports, offline queue, evidence and exports so information does not disappear between WhatsApp, Excel and loose notes.
detail.step1: Coordination loads or syncs appointments and team assignments.
detail.step2: Technicians enter with PIN, see their work and complete the report on mobile.
detail.step3: The app stores evidence and data offline when field connectivity fails.
detail.step4: Coordination exports PDF/Excel and updates project status.
detail.output2: Offline queue to reduce field information loss.
detail.output3: Exports and states useful for coordination and administration.
detail.signal1: Less dependency on chats for critical information.
detail.signal2: Simple technician access without full-account friction.
detail.signal3: Data ready for QA, follow-up and reporting.

fincontrol.title: FinControl
fincontrol.description: Financial control app for our operation: transactions, cost centers, accounts receivable/payable, cash signals and internal reporting.

detail.intro: FinControl organizes the operational finance layer: transactions, projects, cost centers, accounts receivable, accounts payable and internal reports. It does not replace official accounting; it gives daily operational visibility for clearer decisions.
detail.step1: Record income, expenses, accounts and classification by project or cost center.
detail.step2: Track AR/AP and relevant dates for cash-flow visibility.
detail.step3: Dashboard with charts and signals for liquidity, costs and obligations.
detail.step4: Internal PDF reports for administrative review and decision-making.
detail.output1: Operational view of cash, payments, receivables and projects.
detail.output2: Internal reports for administrative and financial discussions.
detail.output3: Data structure ready to grow toward accounting automation.
detail.signal2: Receivables and payables are visible before they hurt.
detail.signal3: Reports are internal and traceable, not decorative.

nas.title: NAS: drop to customer home
nas.description: Bringing the connection from the existing drop in the street to inside the home. Route through garden or facade, building entry, HÜP and terminal equipment mounting where applicable, with final state documentation.

detail.intro: NAS is the work of bringing the connection from the existing drop to the customer home. The essence: garden/exterior route, house entry, HÜP/GF-TA/ONT mounting where applicable and clean closeout documentation.
detail.step1: Review appointment, available drop, garden/exterior route, home access and entry point.
detail.step2: Execute the exterior route: trench or light conduit where applicable, protect microduct/cable and enter through facade or basement.
detail.step3: Mount HÜP, GF-TA and ONT/router where applicable, with termination, splice/connectorization and tidy cabling.
detail.step4: Close with before/after photos, equipment evidence, real blocker if any and status for QA or billing.
detail.output1: Customer report with exterior route, entry point, mounted equipment and QA photos.
detail.output2: Real open points: blocked garden/route, customer absent, missing material, indoor access, damage or activation out of scope.
detail.output3: Technical closeout ready for coordination: installed, partial, blocked or second visit required.
detail.signal1: The drop → garden → home → equipment route is clear and verifiable.
detail.signal2: Photos show both exterior work and indoor equipment mounting.
detail.signal3: Final status supports QA, second-visit planning and billing without inventing anything.

densification.title: Final Customer Connections
densification.description: Closing pending connections in areas where the network is already deployed. We manage appointments, solve access issues, execute the final installation and document the result with clear status: complete, partial or second visit required.

detail.intro: Densification closes customers in already built areas. The focus is organizing appointments, solving access, executing final connections, documenting evidence and making clear what is complete or needs a second visit.
detail.step1: Review address, network availability, previous state and appointment window.
detail.step2: Coordinate customer/crew and prepare the final FTTH work.
detail.step3: Execute final connection, installation or review according to scope and site conditions.
detail.step4: Close with result, evidence, open items and status for follow-up or billing.
detail.output1: Customer/address record with final state or blocker reason.
detail.output2: Closeout evidence and notes for next actions.
detail.output3: Clean base to measure internal progress without publishing sensitive KPIs.
detail.signal1: Failed appointments are documented, not hidden.
detail.signal2: Second visits come from a real state, not memory.
detail.signal3: Each closeout has enough evidence for administrative control.

hero.subtitle: An engineering company held to three principles — and measured by them on every project, in every discipline.

div01.body1: Subcontract engineering for German carriers. We pull cable, blow tubes, splice fibre and activate homes — to spec, on schedule, with the paperwork that survives an audit.
div01.body2: Hard hats, OTDRs, and NE3 / NE4 project leadership across the DACH region. Field engineering that holds up under audit.

div02.body1: One operational stack for telecom field work: production metrics, cost-per-unit, quality control and open tickets — synced in real time to a portal your customer can watch.
div02.body2: Production-grade software sold to carriers and contractors who need real-time control over their networks.

feat.01: Live production dashboards — every segment, every crew.
feat.02: Cost & margin truth, down to the metre.
feat.03: AXON — the AI agent that flags drift before it costs you.

01.title: Survey
01.body: We walk the route, model it to scale, and quote to the metre — no surprises buried in the contract.

02.title: Build
02.body: Crews pull, blow and splice. Every action logged on-site, synced live. You watch progress the moment it happens.

03.title: Activate
03.body: FTTH commissioning and OTDR-verified handover. Splice loss, port maps and as-builts exported in one pack.

04.title: Operate
04.body: We monitor the network around the clock. Faults raise tickets, dispatch the nearest crew, and close the loop — fast.

fibra.subtitle: We execute fibre projects with technical and operational control at every stage. NEXUS doesn't just install: it structures, coordinates, executes, measures, documents and delivers.

01.title: Consultancy & structuring
01.body: We analyse the real project scope, define phases, organise resources and review technical requirements to prepare a clear execution structure with estimated timelines.

02.title: Budgeting
02.body: We prepare budgets based on real scope: civil works, NE3, splicing, blowing, installations, activations, appointments, equipment and operational support. Every line item broken down and justified.

03.title: Appointments & operational coordination
03.body: We manage appointments with clients, technicians, administrators, property owners and site managers. Precise coordination avoids delays and improves field execution.

04.title: Civil works
04.body: Trenching, ducting, conduits and permits. We prepare the route, resolve crossings and chambers, carry out repairs and coordinate with existing infrastructure.

05.title: Outdoor network · NE3
05.body: Fibre routing, main routes and distribution to buildings and delivery points. We leave the outdoor network technically prepared to continue with indoor installation.

06.title: Splicing & measurements
06.body: Fibre joints, enclosures, ODF and CTO. We carry out continuity tests and OTDR measurements with quality control and technical documentation for each point.

07.title: Fibre blowing
07.body: Blowing inside ducts and microducts with route control, cable preparation and route validation. We verify each section before proceeding.

08.title: Installation & activation · NE5
08.body: Final customer installation, equipment connection and ONT/router where applicable. We validate the service, activate the connection and carry out the final functional test.

09.title: Handover & documentation
09.body: Technical closeout with photographic evidence, reports, measurements and final project status. We deliver complete and traceable documentation of the entire execution.

software.subtitle: We design software that converts complex processes into clear, measurable and scalable systems. We don't just code: we analyse, design, structure, develop, integrate, automate and maintain.

01.title: Consultancy & process analysis
01.body: Before coding, we understand the business: workflows, operational problems, users, data, integrations, risks and real objectives. Software starts with the right question.

02.title: Project structuring
02.body: We define scope, modules, priorities, architecture, roadmap, budget, timelines and delivery criteria. A clear plan before writing the first line.

03.title: UX/UI & product design
03.body: Clear interfaces, dashboards, admin panels, forms and internal flows designed for daily use. Design that reduces friction and errors, not adds to them.

04.title: Frontend & backend development
04.body: We build web applications, APIs, databases, internal panels, management systems, portals and operational tools. Software that holds up under real daily use.

05.title: Automation & artificial intelligence
05.body: Bots, assistants and automation of repetitive tasks. Data analysis, report generation, information classification and AI-assisted operational support.

06.title: Integrations
06.body: We connect with CRMs, ERPs, external APIs, internal systems, WhatsApp/Telegram, email, calendars, spreadsheets, payments and the platforms you already use. Everything speaking the same language.

07.title: Testing, security & quality
07.body: Tests, error control, user permissions, validations, traceability and backups. Architecture best practices so the system is reliable and auditable.

08.title: Deploy, maintenance & evolution
08.body: We support launch, monitoring and support. Continuous improvements and scalability: the system grows with your operation instead of holding it back.

cases.heading: Software we already
cases.sub: We don't just design systems for others — we run our own. These are the tools built from real fiber operations.

history.label: Story
history.title: Built from field
history.subtitle: Nexus connects fiber deployment experience with clean documentation and digital tools for operations.
history.intro: HMR Nexus Engineering GmbH comes from practical FTTH work: field coordination, technical evidence, handover and operational control. The goal is simple: less friction between site, project management and client.

principles.field: Field work must stay traceable — from assignment to handover.
principles.documentation: Documentation is not an appendix; it is part of execution.
principles.control: Digital tools are built where workflows need to become repeatable and measurable.

origin.title: Origin
origin.text: The foundation is operational fiber work in Germany and the daily coordination of teams, appointments and evidence.

field.title: Field work
field.text: NE3, NE4, NAS and densification are treated as concrete work scopes with clear states and clean handover.

software.title: Tools
software.text: Software grows from recurring operational problems: appointments, reports, photos, finance and status clarity.

today.title: Today
today.text: Nexus presents itself as a focused partner for FTTH execution, documentation and operational control.
